name: Dolphin 2.5 Mixtral 8x7B
archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (8 experts, top-2 routing; Mixtral 8x7B base)
note: QLoRA fine-tune of Mixtral 8x7B (Apache-2.0) with refusals filtered out and Dolphin-Coder/MagiCoder data added; a benchmark of the late-2023 'uncensored' MoE fine-tune wave.
tpsNote: Q4 (~26GB) slightly exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM; typically run with partial CPU offload or reduced context.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: HF repo dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b exists, fine-tune of Mixtral-8x7B by Eric Hartford / Cognitive Computations, Apache-2.0, base 32K context fine-tuned at 16K, trained with Dolphin-Coder/MagiCoder data and refusals filtered (confirmed on model card and erichartford.com blog). releaseDate corrected/confirmed precisely via HF API createdAt field = 2023-12-14T00:45:47Z, matching the record exactly (training itself ran Dec 9-11, 2023, blog post published Dec 22, 2023). No results rows were present to verify. No changes needed. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): No real, citable benchmark results found for Dolphin 2.5 Mixtral 8x7B (dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b) after a widened search. This is not a Chinese or Japanese vendor (Cognitive Computations / Eric Hartford is an English-language, US-based open-source fine-tuner), so I focused on angle 3 of the brief: Artificial Analysis, LMSYS/Arena, OpenRouter, and Wayback/vendor-page checks.

Checked and found nothing:
- HF model card (huggingface.co/dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b) — no eval numbers, confirmed by direct fetch.
- TheBloke/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b-GGUF quantized model card — no eval numbers, confirmed by direct fetch.
- HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard discussion #517 — Dolphin Mixtral models repeatedly FAILED evaluation (weights not in safetensors format at the time), so no scores were ever produced for this model on that leaderboard.
- OpenRouter — the model page for ehartford/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b returns not-found/no listing (only the later Dolphin 2.6 Mixtral variant is listed under a different org path, cognitivecomputations/dolphin-mixtral-8x7b, which is a different model).
- Artificial Analysis — no dedicated benchmark page for this Dolphin fine-tune; only covers base Mixtral 8x7B Instruct, a distinct model.
- Eric Hartford's own blog post (erichartford.com/dolphin-25-mixtral-8x7b) — training/release details only, no benchmark numbers.
- web.archive.org — direct fetch tool blocked access to archive.org, so Wayback snapshots of the vendor page could not be checked this round.

One dead end explicitly ruled out: telnyx.com/llm-library page and a couple of similar SEO aggregator sites (anakin.ai) claim specific numbers (Arena Elo "1063", MMLU "~70.8%"). On direct fetch and inspection these are confirmed auto-generated/template comparison pages with NO source citation ("community benchmarks" unattributed), internally inconsistent (the same Elo number "1063" is separately attributed in the same text to both Dolphin 2.5 and to Llama 2 Chat 13B), and there is no evidence dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b was ever actually rated in LMSYS Chatbot Arena (it's a niche community fine-tune, not a model LMSYS evaluated). These numbers are not trustworthy and were deliberately NOT reported. A separate anakin.ai page cites an informal Reddit quiz score ("15/18", "13/18") which is not a standard benchmark and has no taxonomy fit.

Conclusion: this appears to be a genuine case of a model whose creator never published standard benchmark results, and whose obsolete quant format prevented it from ever completing Open LLM Leaderboard evaluation. No additions recommended. Suggest updating verificationNotes on the corpus file to record this final, widened-search null result so future rounds don't re-attempt the same dead ends (HF card, TheBloke GGUF card, Open LLM Leaderboard #517, OpenRouter, Artificial Analysis, erichartford.com blog, and the telnyx/anakin.ai low-quality aggregator numbers). ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): No new benchmark results found for Dolphin 2.5 Mixtral 8x7B (dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b, corpus/models/cognitive-computations-eric-hartford/dolphin-2-5-mixtral-8x7b.json) after checking both specifically-requested structured sources plus two supplementary checks. Details:

1. Epoch AI benchmarks data feed — fetched both https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (metadata-only, no per-model rows, ruled out as a wrong-shape source) and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (the real per-model results feed, 5981 rows). Grepped the CSV case-insensitively for "dolphin" (0 matches) and "mixtral" (many matches, but all are base/hosted Mixtral variants only: Mistral AI's own `Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1`, `open-mixtral-8x7b`, and `open-mixtral-8x22b` — none of these are the Dolphin fine-tune, none from cognitivecomputations/ehartford/dphn). Epoch AI has never evaluated this model.

2. Vals.ai — fet... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text]

ROUND-14 (2026-07-16, HF model-card raw-content + academic-citation search, per brief targeting these two specific source types not previously exhausted):

1. HuggingFace model card raw content: fetched the rendered card (huggingface.co/dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b), the raw README (huggingface.co/dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b/raw/main/README.md), the HF API metadata endpoint (huggingface.co/api/models/dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b — "Model Index: None provided", i.e. no model-index/eval_results block in the card's YAML frontmatter), and the Files-and-versions tree listing (huggingface.co/dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b/tree/main — 50 files, all config/tokenizer/weight shards; explicitly confirmed no eval_results.json, evaluation.json, results.json, or similar file present). No benchmark table, no eval metadata. Confirmed null.

2. Academic citation search: this is a community QLoRA fine-tune with no vendor-published paper/arXiv ID (unlike ERNIE 3.0 Titan-style paper-published models), so there is no "original paper" for other work to cite scores against in a comparison table. Attempted Semantic Scholar Graph API search (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search?query=...) three times across ~2 minutes, all three calls returned HTTP 429 Too Many Requests (both via WebFetch and direct curl) — genuine rate-limiting/access failure, not a null result; this source could not actually be queried this round. As a substitute, ran targeted web searches (site:arxiv.org "dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b"; general arXiv search) and found exactly one paper that name-checks the model: arXiv:2404.14230 ("Resistance Against Manipulative AI: key factors and possible actions"), which includes Dolphin-2.5-Mixtral-8x7B as one of several LLMs tested for generating manipulative/persuasive content. Quoted from the paper: "Dolphin (41.67%) achieved similar results" (success rate at generating manipulative hints) and "it was not uncommon for Dolphin to use emotional appeals (pathos), which were the basis of the 37.5% hints it generated." These are custom persuasion/manipulation-generation metrics specific to that paper's own eval harness — not a match to any taxonomy slug (not bfcl, not any standard reasoning/knowledge/coding benchmark) — so not reportable as a corpus result.

Conclusion: both source types from this round's brief were genuinely checked (HF card confirmed null via multiple angles including API metadata and file listing; academic search substituted web/arXiv search for the rate-limited Semantic Scholar API and found only a non-taxonomy custom-metric mention). No additions. Suggest a future round retry the Semantic Scholar API directly (with backoff/API key) as the one sub-source not actually completed this round, though given the model has no vendor paper, a hit is unlikely.

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] Dolphin 2.5 Mixtral 8x7B (dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b) — checked both specified source types, no reportable results found.

(1) HuggingFace model card raw content: model HAS an HF repo (dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b). Fetched the rendered card, the raw README.md, the HF API metadata endpoint, and the Files-and-versions tree listing. Confirmed no evaluation table, no model-index/eval_results in card YAML (API explicitly returns "Model Index: None provided"), and no eval_results.json or similar file among the 50 files in the repo. Genuinely null.

(2) Academic citation search: model has NO vendor-published/arXiv paper (it's a community QLoRA fine-tune, not a paper-published model like ERNIE 3.0 Titan), so there's no canonical paper for others to cite scores against. Semantic Scholar Graph API returned HTTP 429 (rate-limited) on 3 separate attempts across ~2 minutes (both WebFetch and direct curl) — this sub-source could not actually be queried this round, which is an access failure rather than a confirmed null. Substituted targeted arXiv/web search and found one paper mentioning the model by name: arXiv:2404.14230 ("Resistance Against Manipulative AI"), which reports a custom "manipulation generation obedience" rate of 41.67% and pathos-usage rate of 37.5% for Dolphin — these are paper-specific custom metrics, not a match to any approved taxonomy slug (not bfcl/mmlu/gpqa/etc.), so not reportable as a corpus benchmark result.

verificationNotes in the corpus JSON has been updated with a ROUND-14 entry documenting this trail (including the Semantic Scholar 429s) so future rounds don't re-attempt the same HF-card checks, and know the Semantic Scholar API specifically was rate-limited rather than genuinely queried-and-null.

[ROUND 21 — DELTA-CHECK, 2026-07-16] Light re-check only (per brief, since ROUND 20 above is dated the same day with no plausible time gap for new information to appear). Ran two targeted web searches ("dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b" benchmark MMLU score; "Dolphin 2.5 Mixtral" leaderboard 2026) covering source types 1-3 (official materials, independent leaderboards, sibling-model cross-reference). Results returned only the same previously-identified and previously-discredited low-quality aggregator pages (telnyx.com/llm-library, anakin.ai) repeating the same unattributed "MMLU ~70.8%" and "Arena Elo 1063" figures already ruled out in the ROUND-7 note as internally inconsistent, uncited "community benchmark" numbers with no evidence this model was ever actually rated in LMSYS Chatbot Arena. No official model-card update, no new Epoch AI/Vals.ai/LMArena listing, and no new sibling-model (Dolphin 2.6/2.7 Mixtral) cross-reference surfaced. Reaffirming: no new, citable benchmark results exist for this model as of 2026-07-16. All 9 core benchmark slugs (mmlu, gpqa, hle, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu) remain correctly omitted for lack of evidence.
